chunk_summary: This section discusses the selection and use of WHO/IAP growth charts, focusing on preterm infant growth charting with corrected age and the application of growth velocity and Z-scores for monitoring development.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of using corrected age for preterm infant growth charting.
Learn how to apply WHO/IAP growth charts for monitoring growth velocity and Z-scores.
Compare different growth standards, such as INTERGROWTH-21st and Fenton charts.
Identify key guidelines for nutritional assessment and anthropometry in growth monitoring.
key_facts: Corrected age is essential for accurate growth charting in preterm infants.
WHO growth standards provide Z-scores to assess growth velocity and deviations from the norm.
INTERGROWTH-21st and Fenton charts are commonly used for preterm infants, with notable differences in their growth standards.
Growth monitoring should include anthropometric measurements such as weight, length, and head circumference.
The RCPCH guidelines emphasize plotting preterm infant growth using corrected age for up to 2 years.
reference_quote: ‘Corrected age should be used for plotting preterm infant growth until at least 2 years of age.’ (Source: RCPCH guidelines)
